notes: The CMake target tile_example_grouped_gemm_preshuffle exists but was not built in build/bin.
The existing CMake cache was created at /ck/build, so cmake --build through the mounted long path failed.
A standalone binary was compiled under /tmp from unchanged CK-Tile source using the container clang++ and gfx950 target.

correctness.reason: The run used validate=0 and is a throughput probe. Promotion still requires a PyTorch-bound correctness oracle on compact DSv4 tensors.

read: The existing CK-Tile small-M preshuffle path greatly reduces row padding compared with the stock M=128 grouped binary, from 2.0x to 1.118x at S4096. It is nevertheless slower: 8.997 ms versus 4.304 ms for retained padded torch.bmm(out=), only 0.478x of the baseline. The bottleneck is now kernel efficiency for very small M, not only row padding.
next_action: Do not pursue the existing preshuffle decode config as-is. A custom CK candidate should test an intermediate M tile such as 32 or 64 with N=128 or 256, preserve row-major B if possible to avoid a weight-layout detour, expose a PyTorch-callable binding with compact descriptors, validate against the PyTorch compact oracle, and beat the 4.304 ms S4096 W2 dgrad baseline before any full-layer integration.
